title: Map rendering handoff
goal: Introduce rendered ecosystem diagrams without making Leitstand the render authority or hiding the source Mermaid and provenance.

acceptance.assertion: The preferred implementation consumes Cabinet- or Schauwerk-produced SVG/HTML render artifacts with digest/freshness metadata.
If client-side Mermaid rendering is used, the source Mermaid and digest remain visible and render success is not framed as validation.
Rendered diagrams retain links to Cabinet source paths and source commit information.

validation.focused_view_and_controller_tests: 37 passed across 5 files on current Leitstand head
validation.live_service: leitstand.service active/running, NRestarts=0, exact release commit 4218e10ab4ea9dc029c98009f4f142c7bbcc81eb

metadata.outcome: Implemented through Leitstand PR #164 and retained through current production main with later source-alignment, explorer and fullscreen hardening.

verified_acceptance.preferred-handoff: src/controllers/ecosystemMap.ts consumes the Systemkatalog-owned, commit- and SHA-256-bound artifact manifest and verifies all declared artifacts. The current renderer uses the contract-authorized client-side fallback rather than inventing map semantics.
verified_acceptance.fallback-bounded: src/public/ecosystem-map.mjs renders the checked Mermaid source as SVG while src/views/ecosystem-map.ejs keeps the verified Mermaid source and digest available and explicitly states that render success does not validate the map.
verified_acceptance.source-visible: The ecosystem-map view exposes the exact Systemkatalog repository, bound commit, artifact path, source line fallbacks, freshness and non-claims.
